title: Phone-based correlation for Firestore queue (not bot_id)
rationale: The app knows which phone numbers it dispatched to. Firestore docs don't reliably carry bot_id in all cases. Matching by last 11 digits of phone number is more robust and works for both FUP (chapas table) and BID (bid_disparos table).
alternatives: bot_id filter via where('bot_id','in', meusBotIds()) — rejected because bot_id is unreliable and leaks which bots an installation owns.

title: Firebase Web SDK (not firebase-admin) in desktop app
rationale: Desktop app is distributed to end users — embedding a service account private_key would be a security catastrophe. Web SDK with Firestore rules + Anonymous Auth provides sufficient isolation without exposing admin credentials.
alternatives: firebase-admin — rejected, would require embedding private_key in the installer.

title: Collapsed by default: Prioridades de Ação and Confirmações Automáticas
rationale: Both panels are secondary context — operators open FUP Dashboard to act on tasks, not to review priority scores or auto-confirmation logs. Starting collapsed reduces visual noise on open.
alternatives: Expand by default (previous behavior) — rejected by user explicitly.

title: Carteira manual add requires exact Meu Chapa dashboard name
rationale: companyMatches() does fuzzy matching but the BID/FUP dashboards ultimately filter by the stored nome_fantasia. Any deviation from the exact Meu Chapa name causes companies to disappear from dashboards.
alternatives: Auto-match on save — rejected, adds complexity and false-positives risk.

title: Lead Protocol v2.0.4 adopted for MCM
rationale: User wants multi-LLM coordination and persistent session state so no work is lost between context resets. Lead Protocol provides a file-based three-layer state model without requiring external infrastructure.
alternatives: Continue with CLAUDE.md-only approach — rejected by user who explicitly requested Lead Protocol.
